title: Frontend deploy script and GitHub Actions workflow
description: npm run build + aws s3 sync deploy script and GitHub Actions workflow that deploys the React frontend to S3 and invalidates the CloudFront cache on every merge to main.

acceptance_criteria.then: the built frontend files are synced to the S3 bucket and a CloudFront invalidation is created

acceptance_criteria.given: a PR is merged to main
acceptance_criteria.then: it builds the frontend, syncs to S3, and invalidates CloudFront automatically
it sets correct cache headers: max-age=31536000 for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html
AWS credentials come from GitHub secrets, not hardcoded values
the latest version of the dashboard is served

non_functional_requirements.security: AWS credentials must come from GitHub OIDC or secrets — never hardcoded. S3 sync uses --delete flag to remove stale files.
non_functional_requirements.performance: CloudFront invalidation targets /* to clear all cached files after deploy.

definition_of_done: scripts/deploy-frontend.sh builds and syncs to S3
Cache headers set correctly for hashed assets vs index.html
AWS credentials from GitHub secrets only
CloudFront URL serves latest dashboard after deploy
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md and .claude/constitutions/security.md before starting. The S3 bucket name and CloudFront distribution ID must be read from Terraform outputs — not hardcoded in the script. Use --delete flag on aws s3 sync to remove files that no longer exist in the build output.
